mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding labor income and consumption, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth does not positively predict 20-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, further increases represent restrictive monetary policy that tightens financial conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) jointly signal robust demand and labor market health, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint z-scores of RSAFS and UNRATE do not outperform their univariate models in predicting 60-day returns (interaction term t-stat < 2.0 in pooled regression).

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL exceeds 200, month-over-month increases in industrial production (INDPRO) indicate an overheating economy that invites monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL > 200, positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO do not produce negative average 5-day SPX returns (mean forward return >= 0 over 2-year window).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding corporate output and earnings capacity, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO 3-month change fails to positively predict 60-day forward returns (Spearman correlation < 0.03 over 3-year window).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5%, further declines signal a tight labor market that compresses profit margins via wage pressures, generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE < 5%, further declines do not coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns (positive hit rate > 55% over 3-year window).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a high federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and decelerating retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signals restrictive monetary policy hitting consumption, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The bivariate interaction of FEDFUNDS and RSAFS does not improve 20-day return prediction over univariate FEDFUNDS (R-squared increment < 0.01 with F-test p > 0.05 over 2-year window).

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in housing starts (HOUST) signals construction sector strength and upstream demand for materials and labor, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in HOUST do not generate positive average 5-day SPX returns (t-stat of mean return <= 1.5 over 2-year window).
